title: SearchBar component for React filters and command palettes
description: SearchBar and SearchSuggestionList primitives for React and Next.js—styled text field with an optional leading icon slot and a simple suggestion list for site or in-app search.

og.title: Search · Zentauri UI
og.description: SearchBar and SearchSuggestionList primitives for React and Next.js—styled text field with an optional leading icon slot and a simple suggestion list.

twitter.title: Search · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: SearchBar and SearchSuggestionList primitives for React and Next.js—styled text field with an optional leading icon slot and a simple suggestion list.

intro: Use SearchBar for controlled query strings with the same input styling as the rest of the kit. Pair it with SearchSuggestionList when you want a lightweight list of matches—without wiring a full modal or command palette.
useCases: Documentation or marketing site search over static routes.
Filter narrow lists in settings or admin tables.
Prototype command palettes before adding keyboard shortcuts.
Search-as-you-type with your own ranking or API.

faqs.question: Does SearchBar work with the Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use it inside a client component when you need useState for the value; keep surrounding layout in server components if you prefer.

faqs.question: Is a modal required?
faqs.answer: No. SearchBar and SearchSuggestionList are presentational. You can render them inline on a page or wrap them in your own dialog when you need one.

faqs.question: How do I connect SearchSuggestionList to data?
faqs.answer: Filter your items in the parent, map them to { id, label, description?, group? }, and pass them to SearchSuggestionList with an onSelect handler.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
SearchBar wraps a native input with optional leadingSlot (for example a search icon). SearchSuggestionList renders grouped rows as buttons for pointer and keyboard focus.

Neither component fetches data or owns navigation—you control value, filtering, and routing.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Keep filtering logic in the parent or a small hook. Cap result counts for performance on large indexes.

Use consistent ids for list items so selection handlers stay stable.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Provide a visible label or aria-label on SearchBar. For inline suggestion lists, ensure focus order matches user expectations when combining with dialogs.

This preview exposes a single h1 in the hero.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Import from @zentauri-ui/zentauri-components/ui/search. Rebuild the components package after API changes so types and bundles stay in sync.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical and Open Graph URLs resolve on deploy.
